title: narcissus poeticus subsp. hellenicus
intro: Narcissus poeticus subsp. hellenicus, also known as the Hellenic poeticus daffodil, is a subspecies of the Narcissus poeticus plant, characterized by its trumpet-shaped flowers and strong, pungent fragrance. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Narcissus poeticus subsp. hellenicus include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve immune function, and act as an antispasmodic to alleviate muscle cramps and spasms. The plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
